nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving rivers and streams of the Indian subcontinent, where it grows submersed over silty and sandy substrates in soft, gentle current.

decorPreferences: open background space for tall strap-like leaves

summary: Cryptocoryne Retrospiralis is a tall, grass-like aquarium plant from slow rivers and streams in India and Bangladesh. Its long, narrow, ribbon-like leaves typically reach 40 to 50 cm, making it a natural choice for background planting in medium-to-large aquariums. It is closely related to Cryptocoryne spiralis.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne Retrospiralis is a tall, grass-like plant native to slow-moving rivers and streams in India and Bangladesh, closely related to the better-known Cryptocoryne spiralis ([fw-plant-065]). It produces long, narrow, ribbon-like leaves that arch outward from a basal rosette (a cluster of leaves growing from a central base at the substrate level), making it a natural background plant in aquariums 60 cm and longer. Mature specimens commonly reach 40 to 50 cm tall, with some reports of growth approaching 60 cm depending on conditions and strain. It is a heavy root feeder and does best planted into a nutrient-rich substrate supplemented with root tabs to sustain steady growth. Low to moderate light is sufficient, and CO2 injection is beneficial but not required. Care is generally considered easy once the plant is established; the main caution is against relocating a rooted specimen, as moving it can trigger crypt melt — a temporary leaf die-off that occurs when the plant experiences a sudden change in conditions.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary source. Confirmed Indian/Bangladeshi origin, 70-82°F (~21-28°C) temperature, ~20-38 cm height, low-to-moderate light, optional CO2, root-feeder substrate with root tabs, slow-to-moderate growth, midground-to-background placement, easy-to-moderate care level, and crypt-melt risk on shipping/parameter swings.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Secondary source. Confirmed pH 6.5-7.5, 10-24 in (~25-60 cm) height, moderate light, CO2 optional but beneficial, medium growth rate, mid/background placement, runner propagation, easy care, family Araceae, and sensitivity to relocation/strong light. Lists origin as India, China, Thailand — Indian origin matches Modern Aquarium and Wikipedia; the China/Thailand attribution is broader than other sources support and was not adopted.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Cryptocoryne retrospiralis)
additional.notes: Attempted as tertiary corroboration for taxonomy and native range; URL returned HTTP 404 on both attempts and could not be used. Family Araceae / order Alismatales taken from genus-level knowledge and the C. spiralis sibling entry rather than from this source.
